description: An introductory course on Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) — covering core concepts through hands-on implementation. You will learn how RAG addresses the hallucination, recency, and domain-knowledge limitations of LLMs, explore embeddings, vector databases, and chunking strategies, and finally build a working end-to-end RAG pipeline using LangChain.
summary: Starting from why RAG is necessary, you will gain a solid understanding of embedding models, vector databases, and document chunking strategies, then finish by building a fully functional RAG pipeline that covers loading, chunking, embedding, retrieval, and LLM invocation.
target_audience: Developers who have experience using LLMs or generative AI but have not yet built a RAG system from scratch. Familiarity with Python basics and calling SDKs such as OpenAI or LangChain is sufficient.

lectures.title: RAG Overview and Architecture — Why Pair Retrieval with an LLM?
objectives: Explain the limitations of standalone LLMs (hallucination, recency, lack of domain knowledge) and describe how RAG addresses each
Identify the three core components of RAG (Retriever, Augmenter, Generator) and trace the full data flow through the pipeline
Compare RAG with fine-tuning and determine which approach is more appropriate in a given scenario

lectures.title: Embedding Models and Vector Representations — Turning Meaning into Numbers
objectives: Explain how embeddings work and how cosine similarity enables semantic search
Compare major embedding models — OpenAI text-embedding-3, BGE, and SBERT — in terms of dimensionality, quality, and cost trade-offs
Identify the criteria for selecting an embedding model in multilingual environments that include Korean

lectures.title: Comparing and Choosing Vector Databases — Chroma, FAISS, Pinecone, and pgvector
objectives: Distinguish between the operational models of Chroma, FAISS, Pinecone, pgvector, and Weaviate (embedded, managed, and relational-extension)
Understand how ANN index algorithms such as HNSW and IVF affect retrieval speed and accuracy
Select an appropriate vector database for a project by weighing data scale, operational overhead, and cost

lectures.title: Document Loading and Chunking Strategies — chunk_size, Overlap, and Metadata
objectives: Load documents in various formats (PDF, HTML, Markdown) using LangChain document loaders
Understand the differences among Fixed, Sentence, Recursive, and Semantic chunking, and tune chunk_size and overlap accordingly
Preserve metadata such as source and page number alongside chunks to improve retrieval quality and citation accuracy

lectures.title: Building a Basic RAG Pipeline — End-to-End MVP with LangChain
objectives: Build an indexing pipeline with LangChain (or LlamaIndex) that covers loading, chunking, embedding, and ingesting into a vector database
Retrieve semantically similar chunks from a user query, inject them as context into a prompt, and call an LLM
Run a minimal working RAG MVP and perform a basic evaluation of retrieval quality
